project.creator: SonNT
environment: 2. Backend: NestJS wizard and project-requests modules
4. Evidence source: docs/Use_Cases_UC01_UC10.doc, Class Specifications Create Request, client/src/features/wizard/*, server/src/modules/wizard/*
project.notes: Strict feature-based workbook for UC-08 Request Creation Wizard. The repository provides implementation and use-case detail for the five-step wizard, but no executed integration evidence was captured for this flow during this run, so all cases remain Untested.

changeLog.description: Initial feature/function-based workbook generated from the documented use case and the implemented client/server wizard flow.
changeLog.reference: UC-08 use-case specification, wizard page implementation, and wizard service interfaces

features.name: Request Creation Wizard
features.testRequirement: Validate five-step wizard question loading, required step gating, backward navigation, attachment handling, KYC-gated submission, marketplace submission, and private invite-flow creation for UC-08 Request Creation Wizard.

functions.name: Load Wizard Questions
functions.description: The wizard requests active questions from the backend and binds them to the five-step flow in sorted order.
functions.preCondition: Client is authenticated and the wizard questions endpoint is reachable.

functions.name: Navigate Between Wizard Steps
functions.description: The client advances through Product Type, Industry, Budget and Timeline, Features, and Project Details with guarded navigation.
functions.preCondition: Wizard page has loaded and the user is interacting with the step controls.

functions.name: Manage Reference Attachments
functions.description: The final wizard step uploads and deduplicates supporting files before submission.
functions.preCondition: Client has reached Step 5 and selected one or more valid files.

functions.name: Submit Wizard Request
functions.description: The wizard converts the collected answers into a project request payload and submits either marketplace or private-draft creation.
functions.preCondition: Client has completed the required fields and, for submission, KYC is approved.

testCases.description: Load active wizard questions successfully when the request creation page opens.
testCases.procedure: 1. Sign in as a client and open the Request Creation Wizard page.\n2. Wait for the initial page load to complete.\n3. Observe the first step content and question-driven controls.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The page calls GET /wizard/questions.\n2. The backend returns active questions with options sorted by sortOrder.\n3. Step 1 renders Product Type choices from the loaded question set.
testCases.actualResults: Not executed in this run. Repository evidence shows WizardPage requests wizardService.getQuestions() on mount and the server returns active wizard questions ordered by question sortOrder and option sortOrder.
testCases.dependencies: Authenticated client session and reachable wizard/questions endpoint.
testCases.note: Evidence source: WizardPage useEffect, wizardService.getQuestions(), WizardController.getQuestions(), WizardService.getWizardData().

testCases.description: Allow forward navigation from Step 1 only after the client selects a product type.
testCases.procedure: 1. Open the wizard on Step 1.\n2. Observe the Next button before selecting Product Type.\n3. Select a product type and click Next.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. Next is disabled while Product Type is empty.\n2. After a product type is selected, Next becomes enabled.\n3. The wizard advances to Step 2 Industry.
testCases.actualResults: Not executed in this run. Repository evidence shows the footer disables Next when currentStep is 1 and productType is empty, then increments currentStep after a valid selection.
testCases.dependencies: Wizard questions loaded and Product Type question present.
testCases.note: Evidence source: WizardPage footer disable expression and handleNext().

testCases.description: Allow backward navigation so the client can revisit earlier steps and revise selections.
testCases.procedure: 1. Move from Step 1 to at least Step 3.\n2. Click Back.\n3. Continue clicking Back until returning to Step 1.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. Back decrements the current step one screen at a time.\n2. The client can revisit previous inputs.\n3. Back is disabled again on Step 1.
testCases.actualResults: Not executed in this run. Repository evidence shows handleBack() decrements currentStep until 1 and the Back button is disabled when currentStep is 1.
testCases.dependencies: Wizard page loaded and at least one prior step completed.
testCases.note: Evidence source: UC-08 alternative flow AF1 and WizardPage handleBack().

testCases.description: Upload supporting files on Step 5 and append the returned attachment metadata to the draft state.
testCases.procedure: 1. Reach Step 5 Project Details.\n2. Choose one or more valid files from the file picker.\n3. Wait for the upload request to complete.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The client posts the selected files to /project-requests/upload as attachments.\n2. Returned attachment metadata is appended to the local attachments list.\n3. The attachment counter and file list update on screen.
testCases.actualResults: Not executed in this run. Repository evidence shows StepB5 uploads files through wizardService.uploadFiles(files, 'attachments') and updates the attachment list with the returned payload.
testCases.dependencies: Client has reached Step 5 and chosen valid attachment files.
testCases.note: Evidence source: StepB5 handleFileChange() and wizardService.uploadFiles().

testCases.description: Prevent duplicate attachment entries when the same uploaded file metadata is returned more than once.
testCases.procedure: 1. Reach Step 5 Project Details.\n2. Upload the same file twice or receive duplicate attachment URLs in the upload response.\n3. Inspect the attachments list.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The attachments list is filtered by URL.\n2. Each unique attachment appears once in the rendered list.
testCases.actualResults: Not executed in this run. Repository evidence shows StepB5 filters the updated list so only the first occurrence of each attachment URL is retained.
testCases.dependencies: Client has reached Step 5 and upload response contains duplicate URLs or repeated upload attempts occur.
testCases.note: Evidence source: StepB5 nextAttachments.filter(...) deduplication by attachment.url.

testCases.description: Post the completed wizard to the broker marketplace when KYC is approved.
testCases.procedure: 1. Complete all five wizard steps with valid Product Type, Industry, budget, timeline, features, title, and description.\n2. Ensure the client KYC status is APPROVED.\n3. Click Post to Market.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. The wizard builds a payload with status PUBLIC_DRAFT and wizardProgressStep.
2. Title and description are trimmed before submission.
3. The client posts the payload to /project-requests.
4. Success navigates to the client dashboard.
testCases.actualResults: Not executed in this run. Repository evidence shows handleSubmit('marketplace') requires approved KYC, builds a PUBLIC_DRAFT payload, calls wizardService.submitRequest(payload), then navigates to ROUTES.CLIENT_DASHBOARD.
testCases.dependencies: Approved KYC profile, completed wizard data, and reachable project-requests create endpoint.
testCases.note: Evidence source: buildPayload(), handleSubmit('marketplace'), and UC-09 normal flow.

testCases.description: Create a private request and continue into broker invitation flow when KYC is approved.
testCases.procedure: 1. Complete all five wizard steps with valid required data.\n2. Ensure the client KYC status is APPROVED.\n3. Click Find & Invite.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The wizard builds a payload with status PRIVATE_DRAFT.\n2. The payload is posted to /project-requests.\n3. On success the client is redirected to /client/requests/{id}?tab=phase1.
testCases.actualResults: Not executed in this run. Repository evidence shows handleSubmit('invite') creates a PRIVATE_DRAFT request through wizardService.submitRequest(payload) and navigates to the private request details phase.
testCases.dependencies: Approved KYC profile, completed wizard data, and reachable project-requests create endpoint.
testCases.note: Evidence source: buildPayload(), handleSubmit('invite'), and UC-09 trigger/postconditions.

testCases.description: Block final submission actions when the client KYC status is not approved.
testCases.procedure: 1. Open the wizard as a client whose KYC is NOT_STARTED, REJECTED, or PENDING.\n2. Complete the visible form fields until Step 5.\n3. Observe the action buttons and attempt final submission.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. Post to Market and Find & Invite remain disabled when canPublish is false.\n2. The warning banner explains that posting and invites are locked until KYC approval.\n3. No submit request call is sent.
testCases.actualResults: Not executed in this run. Repository evidence shows canPublish is derived from kycStatus === 'APPROVED', both final buttons are disabled when canPublish is false, and a status-specific banner is rendered.
testCases.dependencies: A client account whose KYC status is not APPROVED.
testCases.note: Evidence source: checkKycStatus(), canPublish, warning banner branch, and disabled submit buttons.
